architecturalImpact.summary: Additive validation layer with a targeted refactor to consolidate duplicated constants. No changes to rendering pipeline, audio, or UI. The plan stays within the visual subsystem boundary.

newAbstractions.name: shaderIntegrity.ts
newAbstractions.role: GLSL source parser and runtime cross-reference validator
newAbstractions.reason: Fills a genuine gap — no existing module can parse GLSL declarations or cross-check them against runtime bindings. Directly required by the ticket's acceptance criteria.

newAbstractions.name: shaderRegistry.ts
newAbstractions.role: Single source of truth for attribute specs and uniform definitions across all 3 geometry systems
newAbstractions.reason: The same 23 uniforms are currently duplicated verbatim in pointCloud.ts, particleField.ts, and ribbonField.ts. REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES are also duplicated with subtle differences (particleField omits color). Centralizing eliminates a real maintenance hazard.

modifiedContracts.change: Local REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES replaced with import from shaderRegistry
Local REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES replaced with import from shaderRegistry
Local REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES replaced with import from shaderRegistry
Adds validateShaderIntegrity() call after geometry init and before first render
modifiedContracts.breakingRisk: low — throws on mismatch, which is the desired fail-fast behavior consistent with existing validateShaderCompilation pattern

strengths: Addresses all four acceptance criteria with clearly mapped test cases (T-049-01 through T-049-10)
Builds on existing validation patterns (geometryValidator, shaderErrorCollector) rather than replacing them
Registry refactor eliminates real duplication — 23 uniforms × 3 systems is a maintenance liability
Runtime validateShaderIntegrity() in scene.ts mirrors the existing validateShaderCompilation() pattern, maintaining architectural consistency
Regex-based GLSL parsing is proportionate — the project's shaders are flat declarations with no preprocessor complexity
Test coverage is comprehensive: static analysis, runtime cross-reference, varying consistency, and negative cases

concerns.issue: Three.js built-in handling requires a complete THREEJS_BUILTINS set
concerns.detail: The plan acknowledges this risk. The set must include: position, normal, uv, uv2, color, modelViewMatrix, projectionMatrix, modelMatrix, viewMatrix, normalMatrix, cameraPosition, gl_Position, gl_PointSize, gl_PointCoord, gl_FragColor. Missing any built-in would cause false positives in T-049-04.
concerns.recommendation: Derive the list from Three.js documentation and validate it against the actual shaders during implementation.

concerns.issue: particleField has 4 attributes while pointCloud and ribbonField have 5 (no color attribute)
concerns.detail: The registry must correctly model per-system attribute differences. The plan's PARTICLEFIELD_ATTRIBUTES vs POINTCLOUD_ATTRIBUTES split handles this correctly.
concerns.recommendation: None — already addressed in the plan.

concerns.issue: Uniform initialization with COMMON_UNIFORMS defaultValue must match existing hardcoded values exactly
concerns.detail: If the registry's default values drift from what the geometry systems currently set, behavior could subtly change. The plan's Step 3 (refactor) must be a pure value-preserving extraction.
concerns.recommendation: Diff the extracted registry values against each system's current uniform initialization to confirm exact match before committing.

dependencyAnalysis.upstreamDependency: US-041 (shader system must exist with current shader files and geometry systems)
dependencyAnalysis.downstreamImpact: None — this is a validation/integrity layer that doesn't change the rendering API surface
dependencyAnalysis.importGraph: shaderRegistry.ts ← pointCloud.ts, particleField.ts, ribbonField.ts; shaderIntegrity.ts ← scene.ts, tests

testStrategy.coverage: All acceptance criteria mapped to at least 2 test cases each. Includes positive validation, negative validation (T-049-09), cross-system consistency (T-049-06), and compliance (T-049-10).
testStrategy.missingCoverage: None identified — the 10 test cases cover static analysis, runtime binding, varying consistency, and edge cases.

recommendation: Proceed as planned. The architecture is clean, additive, and well-scoped. The registry refactor pays for itself immediately by eliminating triple-duplicated uniform definitions.
